title: somalign — Refactor long functions into internal helpers
text: BiocCheck warns every function longer 50 lines. Seven functions somalign currently trip check. goal shorten extracting cohesive blocks internal, non-exported helpers (dot-prefixed .somalign_*, @export) warnings clear without enlarging user-facing API — users still see exported functions; new helpers invisible namespace generate help pages. Key constraint: BiocCheck flags functions 50 lines, exported . goal “move one big block one helper” “decompose long function several balanced pieces, comfortably 50 lines.” 90-line internal helper just relocate warning. Second constraint: R executed environment, refactor verified -session read-(free-variable return-contract audit). Running test suite BiocCheck user-side handoff.

title: Algorithm and interpretation

title: Algorithm and interpretation
text: Every new sample xsx_s assigned nearest reference node Euclidean distance: k*=arg⁡mink∥xs−Cref,k∥k^* = \arg\min_k \|x_s - C_{\text{ref},k}\| transport involved. assignment deterministic given reference codebook result use downstream analysis.  Output: old_som_unit = k*, old_som_distance, old_som_distance_threshold, outside_reference_distance, final_status, old_som_label, old_som_label_confidence.

title: Algorithm and interpretation
text: Transport solved codebooks (MM query nodes × KK reference nodes), individual samples, keeping problem dimensionality independent sample count.

title: Algorithm and interpretation
text: transport plan PP minimises: ∑ijPijDij+ε⋅KL(P∥𝐚⊗𝐛)+ρq⋅KL(P𝟏∥𝐚)+ρr⋅KL(P⊤𝟏∥𝐛)\sum_{ij} P_{ij} D_{ij}   + \varepsilon \cdot \mathrm{KL}(P \,\|\, \mathbf{}\otimes\mathbf{b})   + \rho_q \cdot \mathrm{KL}(P\mathbf{1} \,\|\, \mathbf{})   + \rho_r \cdot \mathrm{KL}(P^\top\!\mathbf{1} \,\|\, \mathbf{b}) 𝐚\mathbf{} 𝐛\mathbf{b} query reference node masses. KL marginal penalties relax balance constraint: mass can destroyed rather transported, query node good reference counterpart routes small fraction mass rather forcing artificial match. plan found internal pure-R Sinkhorn scaling implementation.

title: Algorithm and interpretation
text: match_fraction near 1 means node transported mass reference nodes; near 0 means little overlap reference population. Label transfer rejected low-match nodes.

title: Algorithm and interpretation

title: Align a query SOM to a reference SOM — somalign_fit

title: Align a query SOM to a reference SOM — somalign_fit
text: query somalign_query object. reference somalign_reference object. epsilon Entropic regularisation strength. cost matrix normalised median positive entry computing Sinkhorn kernel, epsilon approximately scale- dimension-invariant. Values around 0.5 give meaningful regularisation typical z-scored SOM codebooks; small values (< 0.1) make transport increasingly discrete. normalisation scale stored diagnostics$solver$cost_scale. rho_query Query-side unbalanced mass relaxation. rho_ref Reference-side unbalanced mass relaxation. solver Sinkhorn solver variant. "internal" (default) "auto" use primal-domain scaling iteration. "log_domain" uses numerically stable log-potential variant avoids kernel underflow small epsilon high-dimensional codebooks; slower per iteration tolerates cost/epsilon ratios cause "internal" warn. min_match_fraction Minimum transported fraction required query node label transfer accepted. confidence_threshold Minimum top-label probability required query node label transfer accepted. correction_min_mass Minimum transported node mass required correction shift applied. Corrections also require node match fraction pass min_match_fraction. max_iter Maximum internal Sinkhorn iterations. tol Internal Sinkhorn convergence tolerance. chunk_size Integer. Number samples project per chunk computing nearest reference node. Use Inf NULL chunking (allocates full n_samples x n_nodes matrix). Default 10000L.

title: Align a query SOM to a reference SOM — somalign_fit
text: somalign_fit object.

title: Align a query SOM to a reference SOM — somalign_fit
text: transport plan row sums equal query$node_masses exactly – design. Unbalanced optimal transport allows mass destruction, query mass may absorbed rather transported. Deviation grows lower rho_query / rho_ref values higher epsilon. Use diagnostics$ot$max_row_mass_error quantify deviation given fit; near-balanced data, increase rho_query (e.g. rho_query = 10) enforce tighter marginal constraints. warning emitted automatically 50% query mass destroyed. cost matrix normalised median positive entry Sinkhorn kernel computed. makes epsilon scale- dimension-invariant: value produces degree regularisation regardless number features spread codebook coordinates. raw normalisation factor stored diagnostics$solver$cost_scale.

text: query somalign_query object. reference somalign_reference object. anchor_old Numeric matrix (n_anchors × p). Old-batch measurements anchor samples. Must raw (un-normalized) values units preprocessing pipeline data used train reference. pre-center pre-scale; function applies reference$center reference$scale internally. Also accepts data frame numeric columns. anchor_new Numeric matrix (n_anchors × p). New-batch measurements anchor samples. Must raw (un-normalized) values units preprocessing pipeline anchor_old. pre-center pre-scale; function applies reference$center reference$scale internally. Rows anchor_old anchor_new must correspond biological units. Also accepts data frame numeric columns. rho_anchor Non-negative scalar. Controls strongly anchor pairs bias OT cost. rho_anchor = 0 result equals somalign_fit(). Larger values reduce effective cost anchor-supported node pairs, concentrating transport plan routes. Typical range: 0.5–3. epsilon Entropic regularisation strength (see somalign_fit()). rho_query Query-side unbalanced mass relaxation. rho_ref Reference-side unbalanced mass relaxation. solver Sinkhorn solver variant. See somalign_fit(). min_match_fraction Minimum transported fraction label transfer. confidence_threshold Minimum top-label probability label transfer. correction_min_mass Minimum transported mass node correction. max_iter Maximum Sinkhorn iterations. tol Sinkhorn convergence tolerance. chunk_size Integer. title: Align a query SOM to a reference SOM using anchor sample pairs — somalign_fit_anchored
text: Cost modification. Let \(C\) M×K codebook distance matrix normalised median positive entry (somalign_fit()). anchor pairs projected onto query codebook (old batch) reference codebook (new batch), yielding count matrix \(\) \(A_{kl}\) number anchor pairs whose old measurement maps query node \(k\) new measurement maps reference node \(l\). (query SOM trained new-batch data, projecting old-batch anchor onto identifies query node anchor occupied batch shift; projecting new-batch anchor onto reference SOM identifies corresponding reference node shift.) modified cost $$\tilde{C}_{kl} = \max\!\bigl(C_{kl} - \rho_{\mathrm{anchor}} \cdot   A_{kl} / n_{\mathrm{anchors}},\; 0\bigr).$$ Pairs many anchor observations get cost reduced toward zero (free transport), uncovered pairs retain original cost. Non-negativity enforced \(\max(\cdot, 0)\) clamp. Clamp behaviour large rho_anchor. anchor bonus exceeds \(C_{kl}\), effective cost clamped zero. pairs identical effective cost transport mass among determined entropic regularisation alone rather relative anchor counts. clamp required keep costs non-negative; large rho_anchor plan anchor-covered pairs becomes entropic, concentrated. practical upper bound rho_anchor * max() / n_anchors <= 1, .e., even -supported pair reduces cost one median-distance unit. Fallback uncovered nodes. Query nodes anchor samples retain original pairwise costs, transport plan nodes determined entirely OT objective — somalign_fit(). Inspect $anchors$coverage_fraction see fraction query nodes least one anchor pair. Return value. object class c("somalign_anchored_fit", "somalign_fit"), downstream functions accept somalign_fit object (somalign_results(), somalign_diagnostics()) work unchanged. additional $anchors list element attached: n_anchors Number anchor pairs supplied. rho_anchor value rho_anchor used. nodes_covered Number query nodes ≥ 1 anchor pair. coverage_fraction nodes_covered / nrow(query$codebook).

title: somalign 0.99.2
text: somalign_fit() gains solver = "log_domain" option. log-domain Sinkhorn variant works log-potentials ff (query) gg (reference) rather primal scaling vectors, avoiding exp(-C/epsilon) kernel underflow entirely. slower per iteration tolerates cost/epsilon ratios cause default "internal" solver warn. somalign_som_stability() — new function sweeps vector random seeds query SOM training, holds reference fixed, returns per-seed summary data frame. Reports transport_mass, mean_match_fraction, max_row_mass_error, accepted_label_fraction, outside_direct_fraction, outside_corrected_fraction, mean_correction_norm, converged seed, quantifying run--run variance SOM training randomness — largest uncontrolled variance source somalign workflow.

title: somalign 0.99.2
text: somalign_fit() diagnostics now include two scale-invariant marginal-error fields: diagnostics$solver$rel_marginal_row_error diagnostics$solver$rel_marginal_col_error. normalise absolute marginal violations total query reference mass respectively, making easier distinguish expected mass destruction solver non-convergence.

title: somalign 0.99.2
text: vignette("algorithm") gains new Limitations OT correction section covering barycentric contraction (correction target conditional mean contracts toward dense reference nodes grows epsilon), unpaired OT design (mass distributions, individual samples, matched — anchor-based methods exploit sample-level pairing), uncalibrated novelty score (match_fraction reflects epsilon/rho regime much true novel population).

title: somalign 0.99.1
text: somalign_fit() default epsilon changed 0.05 0.5. cost matrix now normalised median positive entry Sinkhorn kernel computed, making epsilon scale- dimension-invariant across feature sets different size spread. Users set epsilon explicitly relative raw codebook distances re-tune normalised scale (inspect diagnostics$solver$cost_scale).

title: somalign 0.99.1
text: somalign_query() gains codebook_space argument (default "reference_scaled"). user-supplied som_query provided, setting codebook_space = "raw" instructs somalign_query() re-scale codebook reference-scaled space, mirroring existing behaviour somalign_reference(). somalign_fit() diagnostics now include diagnostics$solver$converged (logical), diagnostics$solver$final_delta (final iterate change), diagnostics$solver$cost_scale (cost normalisation factor).

title: somalign 0.99.1
text: somalign_fit() now emits warning 50 % query mass destroyed transport, 50 % query samples fall outside reference distance thresholds. non-finite Sinkhorn iterate delta now triggers explicit warning instead finishing silently (previously, NaN delta suppressed early-break condition non-convergence warning). somalign_reference_from_nodes() now emits informational messages label probabilities distance quantiles supplied, users aware label transfer outside-reference detection disabled. internal OT input validator now warns query reference node masses zero, preventing silent -zero transport plan. Fixed silent fallback .somalign_thresholds(): requested distance-quantile column absent, warning now emitted rather silently substituting third column position.

title: somalign 0.99.0
text: Added somalign_train_reference() somalign_reference() train wrap existing kohonen SOM fixed reference object, including node mass, per-node label probability, per-node distance quantile summaries. Added somalign_reference_from_nodes() reconstruct reference object pre-computed node-level artifacts (codebook, center, scale, masses, label probabilities, distance quantiles) without needing original data. Added somalign_query() scale query data reference parameters, optionally train query SOM, return somalign_query object containing per-sample node assignments distances. Added somalign_fit() align query SOM fixed reference SOM via codebook-level unbalanced entropic optimal transport. Uses internal pure-R generalized Sinkhorn solver; "auto" retained compatibility alias internal solver. Added somalign_results() extract per-sample data frame direct reference projection columns (canonical) transport-corrected projection columns (auxiliary, annotation visualisation). Added somalign_diagnostics() return solver, OT plan, node-level, projection diagnostics somalign_fit object. Added somalign_sensitivity_grid() sweep grid OT hyperparameters (epsilon, rho_query, rho_ref) return summary data frame tuning stability assessment. Supports optional parallelisation via BiocParallel::bplapply() parallel = TRUE.
